generated_comment:
Sanjay: I’m all for being supportive, but yaar, aise policies need balance also. If we start giving separate period leave and then long maternity breaks, team planning and project allocation become a real issue — company has to absorb that cost, and men don’t get any equivalent flexibility. 
B: Haan, fairness angle toh management rarely discusses.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s biology and maternity as a workplace liability and treats accommodations as unfair special treatment. It encodes the fairness/economic-cost framing and reverse discrimination angle common in menstrual and maternity leave bias.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 44. A regional director at a large FMCG company in Delhi. You are on a phone call with your counterpart in another region after the company announced that a woman has been appointed as the new national sales head. You are sharing your honest reaction to the appointment and what you think it means for the company's direction.
